rationale: Both correctly diagnose the run-level mismatch (default core_model → dummy weights) matching the actual 'invalid' resolution, and both cite the same core files (run_args.py, stage_config.py's _add_dummy_load_format, the full_model marker). X goes further by tracing the fixture chain (runtime.py resolving tiny models/config swap) and the assertion helpers, and adds practical completeness (hardware/video-path caveats, a constructive footgun-guard suggestion) that better serves a maintainer closing the issue. Y is tighter and includes clickable GitHub links, but is a shade less thorough in tracing the full mechanism. Both share an unverifiable 'Welsh gibberish' audio detail not present in the issue body, capping grounding scores similarly for each.
